summary: Productionised the 3 atlas health checks (falsifier / bridge / status_all) as reusable bash scripts + cron-friendly wrapper + 3 nexus CLI subcommands. All 3 scripts emit raw 80 sentinels, append JSONL to state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, follow raw 71 (report-only never auto-mutate) and raw 66 (reason+fix trailers on errors).

axes.self_host: no — pure bash + python3 for safe JSON cmd extraction (per memory feedback_hexa_sim_falsifier_parser_quirks.md)
axes.first_run: __FALSIFIER_HEALTH__ PASS total=24 clean=24 hit=0 error=0 duration_ms=14000 (F1-F12 + F19-F30, all CLEAN, F12=6.4s outlier as documented in 2026-04-26_falsifier_health_check.md)
axes.tier_1_evidence: 24/24 CLEAN first run; classifier identical to ad-hoc procedure; timeline JSONL line shape matches prior manual entry

axes.self_host: yes (intentional) — invokes hexa for the 16 bridge --selftest paths via gtimeout 30
axes.first_run_note: Initial run hit a sentinel-classifier gap: 9 bridges use [<name>_bridge selftest] OK pattern (codata/horizons/cmb/nanograv/icecube/nist_atomic/gaia/lhc/pubchem) instead of the __NAME_BRIDGE__ PASS pattern (oeis/wikipedia/simbad/openalex/gw/arxiv/uniprot). Single-line regex extension covered both patterns; 2nd run 16/16 PASS. Retains delta vs the 2026-04-26_bridge_health_check.md run (which reported 9/16 PASS due to docker-curl absence) — improvement attributable to subsequent dockerfile_curl_patch ω-cycle landing.
axes.tier_1_evidence: 16/16 PASS, dual sentinel patterns now detected, OFFLINE-FALLBACK count tracked (3 detected in this run)

axes.self_host: no — only invokes the 2 sister bash scripts + atlas_status_all.sh
axes.first_run: F: 24/24 CLEAN | B: 16/16 PASS | S: status_all_ok\n__HEALTH_CHECK_ALL__ PASS falsifier=24/24 bridge=16/16 status=ok
axes.tier_1_evidence: all 3 subchecks green, single sentinel for cron consumption, per-subcheck timeline entries preserved

cli_wiring.wiring_verified: HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa run cli/run.hexa hexa-sim falsifier-health --quiet → __FALSIFIER_HEALTH__ PASS total=24 clean=24
cli_wiring.docker_cage_caveat: Without HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 the parent hexa enters the docker hard-landing container, where bash subprocess paths resolve inside the container (not host). Per 2026-04-25 mac_hard_landing policy, this is by-design; cron should call the bash scripts directly (not via nexus CLI) to avoid container indirection.

timeline_append.schema_falsifier: {ts, scope=falsifier_registry, total, clean, hit, error, duration_ms, checker=falsifier_health.sh}
timeline_append.schema_bridge: {ts, scope=bridge_registry, total, pass, fail, offline_fallback, duration_ms, checker=bridge_health.sh}
timeline_append.schema_status_all: (unchanged — atlas_status_all.sh continues its existing append pattern)

compliance.raw_71: all 3 scripts are report-only — no mutation of registry/bridges/atlas
compliance.raw_66: error paths emit reason+fix trailers (usage error, missing registry, subcheck fail)
compliance.raw_77: registry/bridge files read-only; only state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl is appended
compliance.bash_3_2: no ${var^^}, no [[ =~ ]] used in critical paths, no associative arrays — verified on macOS

fixpoint_marker.byte_eq_seal: n/a (timeline grows monotonically; sentinel surfaces are deterministic given identical state)
fixpoint_marker.idempotence_check: running each script twice produces identical sentinels (modulo ts + duration_ms); 24/24 + 16/16 stable across both invocations this session

next_cycles.description: Wire health_check_all.sh into cron / launchd. Sample crontab line for hourly run:\n  0 * * * * HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/health_check_all.sh --quiet >>/tmp/health_check_all.log 2>&1\nFor launchd, create ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.nexus.health-check-all.plist with ProgramArguments=[/bin/bash, /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/health_check_all.sh, --quiet] + StartCalendarInterval Hour=*/1. Per memory feedback_macos_launchagents_eperm.md, claude code's sandbox blocks ~/Library/LaunchAgents writes — user must place the plist manually (or use the hexa escalator daemon if path is whitelisted).
next_cycles.rationale: scripts are cron-ready (low stdout noise, all detail in JSONL timeline, deterministic sentinels). Not auto-installed: launchd/cron entry creation is a user-controlled action requiring explicit authorization.

next_cycles.description: After N hourly runs, harvest atlas_health_timeline.jsonl scope=bridge_registry rows and surface any pass-count drop ≥2 as a warning in atlas_status_all.sh. Currently bridge sentinel emits binary PASS/FAIL — drift detection lives at the timeline-aggregator layer.

F12 (triple-source-n6-anchor-corroboration) takes ~6.4s — 47% of total wall. Optional: split into F12a/F12b/F12c when registry exceeds 30 entries. Documented as P3 in 2026-04-26_falsifier_health_check.md.
